content.full_text:
78

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 13, 1993

Margherita Hunt Mazur
10085 Grandview Dr.
La Mesa, CA 91941

Re: Your Request For Confirmation
Of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-93-257

Dear Ms. Mazur:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/ Because your question is a general
inquiry, we are treating your letter as a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18239(c).2/

QUESTION

Must a candidate or committee be identified as the sender of
a mailing when campaign material is included in a commercial mail
packet?

CONCLUSION

The candidate or committee is not required to be identified
as the sender on the outside of the envelope on a commercial mail
packet. It is sufficient to identify the candidate or committee
on the campaign material.

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Margherita Hunt Mazur
I-93-257
page 2

ANALYSIS

Section 84305 requires that any "mass mailing" (over 200
pieces of mail sent in a calendar month) sent by a candidate or
committee must contain, on the outside of the mailing, the name
and address of the sender in no less than 6-point type.3/ The
sender of the mass mailing is the candidate or committee who pays
for or incurs the largest portion of expenses attributable to
designing, printing, or posting of the mailing. (Regulation
18435.)

However, with regard to inclusion of political materials
along with non-political materials in a commercial mail packet,
staff has advised that the mass mailing identification need only
appear on the political materials and need not be included on the
outside of the mailing. It would not be practical or reasonable
to require the candidate's or committees's name and address to be
printed on the outside of a commercial mail packet. Therefore,
staff had advised in these situations that the identification
should be printed on the political materials only. (Bagatelos
Advice Letter, No. I-88-383.)

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

_________________________________

3/ When there is more than one sender, the identification must
also appear on the inside of the mailing. (Section 84305.)
